title: quercus × cazanensis
intro: Quercus × cazanensis, also known as the Cazan oak, is a hybrid species of oak tree that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark and leaves of the Cazan oak have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and improving cardiovascular health. The leaves and bark of the Cazan oak can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments.
